LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AUG-22-2018 HEARING ON PLAINTIFF SHU XU'S DEMURRER TO ALL 34 AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES IN THE ANSWER FILED BY DEFENDANT NGCW, INC. AND THE WESTERN SURETY COMPANY. COURT ADOPTED TENTATIVE RULING. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE 34 AND OVERRULED AS TO ALL OTHER AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE 34 PURPORTING TO RESERVE A RIGHT TO ADD ADDITIONAL AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES WITHOUT LEAVE OF COURT IS INVALID. IT IS PREMATURE TO DETERMINE WITH CERTITUDE WHETHER PLAINTIFF IS CORRECT THAT SOME OR ALL OF THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES ARE INAPPLICABLE TO ANY OF THE CLAIMS ASSERTED BY THE PLAINTIFF. AT THIS EARLY STAGE OF THE CASE IT IS NOT POSSIBLE TO DETERMINE WHETHER DEFENDANTS WILL BE ABLE TO DEVELOP ANY FACTUAL SUPPORT FOR ANY OF THESE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. WHILE DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT ALLEGED ANY FACTS IN SUPPORT OF THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, SUCH CONCLUSORY PLEADING OF AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES COMPORTS WITH LONGSTANDING PRACTICE IN BAY AREA STATE COURTS. THE APPROPRIATE COURSE FOR A PLAINTIFF WHEN FACED WITH AN ARRAY OF AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES MANY OF WHICH APPEAR TO BE UNSUPPORTED AND/OR INSUPPORTABLE IS NOT TO BRING A PLEADING MOTION, BUT INSTEAD TO SERVE DISCOVERY SUCH AS FORM INTERROGATORY 15.0 AND, AFTER THE DEFENDANT HAS HAD ADEQUATE TIME TO CONDUCT FACTUAL INVESTIGATION AND TAKE DISCOVERY OF ITS OWN, CONFER WITH THE DEFENDANT TO DETERMINE WHICH, IF ANY OF THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES DEFENDANT INTENDS TO PURSUE AND, IF THERE IS A DISPUTE AT THAT TIME, PLAINTIFF CAN BRING A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON PLEADINGS OR A MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DIRECTED TO THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES THAT ARE TRULY IN DISPUTE. COUNSEL FOR NGCW AND WESTERN SURETY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. (302/HEK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-10-2019 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-30-2019 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2018 Set For Trial On Jun-03-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE GENERAL DENIAL
Off Calendar Aug-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

There being no appearances, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Shu Xu's motion to strike affirmative defenses 4, 8, 9, 11, 12, 15, 16, 17, 19, 20, 21, 23, 24, 27, 29 and 34 in the answer filed by defendant NGCW, Inc. and The Western Surety Company to the first amended complaint is granted as to affirmative defense 34 and denied as to all other affirmative defenses. Affirmative defense 34 purporting to reserve a right to add additional affirmative defenses without leave of court is invalid. It is premature to determine with certitude whether plaintiff is correct that some or all of the affirmative defenses are inapplicable to any of the claims asserted by the plaintiff. At this early stage of the case it is not possible to determine whether defendants will be able to develop any factual support for any of these affirmative defenses. While defendants have not alleged any facts in support of their affirmative defenses, such conclusory pleading of affirmative defenses comports with longstanding practice in Bay Area state courts. The appropriate course for a plaintiff when faced with an array of affirmative defenses many of which appear to be unsupported and/or insupportable is not to bring a pleading motion, but instead to serve discovery such as form interrogatory 15.0 and, after the defendant has had adequate time to conduct factual investigation and take discovery of its own, confer with the defendant to determine which, if any of the affirmative defenses defendant intends to pursue and, if there is a dispute at that time, plaintiff can bring a motion for judgment on pleadings or a motion for summary adjudication directed to the affirmative defenses that are truly in dispute. Counsel for NGCW and Western Surety is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is compliant with CRC 3.1312.
The proposed submitted by email was rejected for non-compliance with CRC 2.100 - 2.119.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO GENERAL DENIAL
Off Calendar Aug-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

There being no appearances, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Shu Xu's demurrer to all 34 affirmative defenses in the answer filed by defendant NGCW, Inc. and The Western Surety Company is sustained without leave to amend as to affirmative defense 34 and overruled as to all other affirmative defenses. Affirmative defense 34 purporting to reserve a right to add additional affirmative defenses without leave of court is invalid. It is premature to determine with certitude whether plaintiff is correct that some or all of the affirmative defenses are inapplicable to any of the claims asserted by the plaintiff. At this early stage of the case it is not possible to determine whether defendants will be able to develop any factual support for any of these affirmative defenses. While defendants have not alleged any facts in support of their affirmative defenses, such conclusory pleading of affirmative defenses comports with longstanding practice in Bay Area state courts. The appropriate course for a plaintiff when faced with an array of affirmative defenses many of which appear to be unsupported and/or insupportable is not to bring a pleading motion, but instead to serve discovery such as form interrogatory 15.0 and, after the defendant has had adequate time to conduct factual investigation and take discovery of its own, confer with the defendant to determine which, if any of the affirmative defenses defendant intends to pursue and, if there is a dispute at that time, plaintiff can bring a motion for judgment on pleadings or a motion for summary adjudication directed to the affirmative defenses that are truly in dispute. Counsel for NGCW and Western Surety is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. The proposed submitted by email was rejected for non-compliance with CRC 2.100 - 2.119.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)
